publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-15 18:25:41 -0400.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Collection contains audio and visual media in assorted formats, awards, a harmonium, Yonjan's notebooks, photographs, along with other documentation of Yonjan's work as a musician, singer, and composer.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gopal Yonjan papers, #8534. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gopal Yonjan was born in Okaytee Tea Estate in Mirik in Darjeeling on August 26, 1943. He became a prominent popular singer and composer of Nepali music. He was also known for his patriotic Nepali songs. His voice range allowed him to sing songs of every genre of Nepalese music. He belongs to the first generation of Nepali singers who became professional singers. His songs have also been used in several movies and dramas across the country. He passed away in Kathmandu in May 20, 1997.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Mold present in open-reel tapes stored in boxes 1-2. Individuals with weak immune systems may need to take extra precautions.

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings on Gopal Yonjan life and musical works scrapbook
Tributes to Gopal Yonjan clippings scrapbook
Nepal Motion Pictures Association - Best Music Director 2045
Nepal's 1st San Miguel Sports Award plaque

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documentary. Excerpts taken from Sagarmatha to be used as Background Music.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recording of a program in Darjeeling organized to raise funds for building a Buddhist monastery in Bodh Gaya.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recording of a program in Darjeeling organized to raise funds for building a Buddhist monastery in Bodh Gaya.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interviews produced by Renchin Yonjan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Classical Guitarist & Composer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special program on Gopal Yonjan from Channel Nepal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Senior Actor; ex Chairperson of Film Artistes Association; started her career as a Folk Dancer at Rastriya Naach Ghar (National Dance Theatre) and worked with Gopal Yonjan at the Police Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ex DIG (Deputy Inspector General of Police). Coordinated programs organized by the Police Club at which Gopal Yonjan was the Music Director and Composer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chairperson of Olympic Committee. Worked with Gopal Yonjan at different National & Regional Sports Meets. Was also a producer of Film Ranko

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ex Police Junior Officer. Worked with Gopal Yonjan for over 15 years at the Police Club. Currently owns a Manpower recruiting agency.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Economist. Retired Professor of Economics at Tribhuvan University. Currently runs a Consulting Firm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professor of Music at Padma Kanya Campus. Worked with Gopal for a short time.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Songs Andho (GY 04) ; Lahure (GY 03); Bachi Dinchu (GY 20) & Samar (GY 08). Nepal Television.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composer, Musician and Music Teacher. Knew Gopal from childhood and played with him during recordings at Radio Nepal. Interviews with Prakash Gurung on two separate occasions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composer, Musician and Music Teacher. Knew Gopal from childhood and played with him during recordings at Radio Nepal. Interviews with Prakash Gurung on two separate occasions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composer, Musician and Music Teacher. Knew Gopal from childhood and played with him during recordings at Radio Nepal. Interviews with Prakash Gurung on two separate occasions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Music Composer currently residing in Mumbai. Interview was taken after Ranjit Gazmer attended the Lyrical Dances program based on many of Gopal's dancing melodies included in the album Gopalaya - Something Old Something New

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sitarist well known in Nepal and Europe. Continues to give sitar lessons. Worked with Gopal Yonjan at Padma Kanya Campus and recorded for Gopal at Radio Nepal and the Police Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Singer and composer. Is influenced greatly by Gopal Yonjan's compositions and says he learnt music by listening to Gopal Yonjan's songs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pop Arttiste. Studied singing at St. Xavier's with Gopal Yonjan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program organized to commemorate Gopal Yonjan's Nationalistic songs. Program was telecast by Nepal Television and contents could be promo plus program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recording of Nishani 53 in Bombay.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Songs from Geet Manjari 1 & 2_ Sangeet, Katus, Bhaag Guna, Suna Ghadi, Sada Basa, Janma Din, Hasana Bhai

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dus Aunla, Lobhi Biralo, Phul Jasto, and Mata Janani.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Satya Bola, Kamila, Vidyarthi, Aakash Ganga, Raat (from telefilm for socia awareness about HIV Aids) and Ma Phul Thiye.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recordings from the album Nishani 52, plus Ma Rojchu Ramro.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chyai Rogi & Pirati from Nishani 52

did.unittitle: Workshop - Technology Transfers, afternoon closing session

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Home recorded cassette housed in Nishani 53 case.
